answer:
The verse work is **Cynewulf's *Elene*** (work slug: `cynewulf-elene-d2d132`), and the described closing passage appears at the end of **Chapter 1**.[^1]

The 'appled gold' mead-hall line reads: "though in the mead-hall he received treasures, **appled gold**."[^2]

The three-tier fire-judgment passage reads: "Then into the fire’s grasp he will divide every person into three... The **righteous will be uppermost in that fire**, the blessed company... The **sinful**, mixed with malice, will be tormented **in the middle**, the heart-sorrowful men, in the hot surge, **covered with soot**. The third part, the **accursed** evil-doers, will be **in the fire’s depths**... but they will be cast from that battle-heat **into hell’s abyss**."[^3]

[^1]: Cynewulf, *Elene*, [Chapter 1](/works/cynewulf-elene-d2d132/01/translation/).

[^2]: Cynewulf, *Elene*, [paragraph](/works/cynewulf-elene-d2d132/01/translation/#p-8f8a1b).

[^3]: Cynewulf, *Elene*, [paragraph](/works/cynewulf-elene-d2d132/01/translation/#p-dcbbfe).
